calendar.matters:
Verified Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee And For Instructions To Determine Entitlement To Estate Distribution.
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Byrne for (petitioner) Ronald Ferguson.

Mr. Byrne addressed the examiner's notes, that in regards to #1 he clarifies that the 1998 trust was a joint marital trust, that in 2010 Harold divided it into 2 sub trusts, that the settlement agreement exhibit F divides the estate, that the 1998 trust owns 77% of the 19th Avenue property, that the balance of the estate are the only assets in the 2010 trust, that they are missing certain pages in the 1998 trust, that they are not revoking it, that they need a trustee and to sell the property and that everything goes to Ms. Koc. Mr. Byrne addressed #2 stating that they are only seeking minimal powers, the authority to show and sell the property, to carry insurance, to file taxes and for tax elections to minimize taxes. Mr. Byrne addressed #3 stating that it is a repeat of #1, that the examiner assumes all property is in the 2010 trust but it is not and that only 24% of the 19th Avenue property is in the trust. The Court took the petition for appointment of successor trustee, etc., under submission and requested that counsel submit a clean order with the proposed requested powers.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
